description: Wire the Knowledge Agent CLI (src/knowledge/cli.ts) to use real neo4j-driver calls and real imports from the existing v3.4 modules.
WHAT EXISTS (v3.4 — already built and passing):
The v3.4 branch (ralph/vega-v3.4-knowledge-agent) contains 19 user stories (US-500 through US-518) with 650 tests passing. All of the following modules exist and are tested:
- src/knowledge/neo4j.ts — Neo4j driver wrapper (initNeo4j, runCypher, applySchema)
- src/knowledge/model-router.ts — ModelRouter (frontier: grok-4-1-fast-reasoning via xAI, local: qwen3:32b via Ollama)
- src/knowledge/vault-connector.ts — VaultConnector (reads Obsidian vault, watches for changes)
- src/knowledge/migration/parser.ts — FrontmatterParser (detects template type from YAML frontmatter)
- src/knowledge/migration/entity-mapper.ts — EntityMapper (Entity notes → Neo4j Entity nodes)
- src/knowledge/migration/person-mapper.ts — PersonMapper (Person notes → Neo4j Entity nodes with entity_type: 'person')
- src/knowledge/migration/account-mapper.ts — AccountMapper (Account notes → Neo4j Entity nodes with BELONGS_TO Institution)
- src/knowledge/decomposition/decomposer.ts — ClaimDecomposer (unstructured text → atomic claims via frontier model)
- src/knowledge/decomposition/contradiction.ts — ContradictionDetector (finds conflicting claims, creates OpenQuestion nodes)
- src/knowledge/aqm/reranker.ts — Stage 3: scores results (semantic_similarity×0.4 + truth_tier×0.35 + recency×0.25)
- src/knowledge/aqm/synthesizer.ts — Stage 4: grounded synthesis with citations
- src/knowledge/proactive/context.ts — ProactiveContext (MOCs, domain summaries, pre-context packages)
- src/knowledge/operational-tables.ts — PostgreSQL table creation (6 tables, idempotent)

The CLI entrypoint (src/knowledge/cli.ts) was written as a scaffold with mock/placeholder implementations. Every function uses Math.random() for counts, hardcoded mock objects, and '// In production:' comments where real imports should go. This PRD replaces ALL mock implementations with real imports from the modules listed above.
The CLI must NOT introduce new logic — it should purely wire existing, tested modules into the CLI command handlers. Think of this as plumbing, not construction.
- Zod for all runtime validation of inputs/outputs at CLI boundaries
- Non-blocking error handling: try/catch, return null on failure, log errors to stderr
- pg ESM import pattern: import pg from 'pg'; const { Pool } = pg;
- neo4j-driver ESM import pattern: import neo4j from 'neo4j-driver';
- All environment variables from process.env (loaded by Apple Keychain via .zshrc)
- vitest with fileParallelism: false for all tests
- Telemetry: all operations emit telemetry events to stderr
- Exit codes: 0 = success, 1 = error, 2 = partial success (Phase 1 criteria not met)
Current state: 1,840 claims migrated from Obsidian vault
Stories are ordered so that each story's dependencies are satisfied by prior stories.
US-600 (initNeo4j) and US-606 (initVaultConnector) are the two foundation stories — everything else depends on one or both.
US-603 (migrate) and US-605 (watch) depend on BOTH Neo4j AND VaultConnector, so US-606 must come before them.

userStories.title: Wire initNeo4j to use real neo4j-driver
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the CLI's initNeo4j function to use the real neo4j-driver package and the existing neo4j.ts module so that all CLI commands operate against the live Neo4j database instead of a mock object.
acceptanceCriteria: Import initNeo4j (or equivalent) from src/knowledge/neo4j.ts instead of using the inline mock
The neo4j-driver package is used via: import neo4j from 'neo4j-driver'; const driver = neo4j.driver(uri, neo4j.auth.basic(user, password));
driver.verifyConnectivity() is called and awaited to confirm the connection before returning
On connection failure, logs to stderr and returns null (non-blocking error handling pattern)
The returned driver object (or session factory) is passed to all downstream command functions
Remove the mock object return: { connected: true, uri, user }
Remove all '// In production:' comments in initNeo4j — this IS production
Test: call initNeo4j with valid credentials → verify driver.verifyConnectivity resolves
Test: call initNeo4j with invalid credentials → verify null returned, error logged to stderr
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the foundation — every other story depends on having a real Neo4j driver. The neo4j.ts module from v3.4 already wraps the driver; we just need to import and use it. If neo4j.ts exports a different interface than what cli.ts expects, adapt cli.ts to match the existing module's API.

userStories.title: Wire initVaultConnector to use real VaultConnector module
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the CLI's initVaultConnector function to use the real VaultConnector from src/knowledge/vault-connector.ts so that vault operations use the tested filesystem integration. Depends on: US-600.
acceptanceCriteria: Import VaultConnector from src/knowledge/vault-connector.ts
Replace the mock initVaultConnector with real VaultConnector instantiation
VaultConnector takes vault path as constructor argument
Call VaultConnector.initialize() (or equivalent) to verify vault is accessible
On failure (vault path doesn't exist, permissions error), log to stderr and return null
Remove the mock return: { connected: true, vaultPath }
Vault path auto-detection: use process.env.VAULT_PATH if set, otherwise default to ~/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/Lingelpedia
Add VAULT_PATH to the Zod EnvSchema as optional with the iCloud default
Test: init VaultConnector with valid vault path → verify connected
Test: init VaultConnector with nonexistent path → verify null returned, error logged
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This parallels US-600 but for the vault side. Both initNeo4j and initVaultConnector are the two foundation pieces every command needs. Making VAULT_PATH configurable via env var is a small improvement — lets us test against a different vault directory without hardcoding. MOVED TO POSITION 2 (was position 7) because US-603 (migrate) and US-605 (watch) depend on VaultConnector being wired first.

userStories.title: Wire cmdStatus to run real Cypher queries for node counts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the status command to query real Neo4j node counts, relationship counts, and latest claim timestamp via Cypher so that the dashboard reflects the actual state of the knowledge graph. Depends on: US-600.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace all Math.random() mock counts with real Cypher queries
Node counts query: MATCH (n) RETURN labels(n)[0] AS label, count(n) AS count ORDER BY count DESC
Relationship count query: MATCH ()-[r]->() RETURN count(r) AS count
Latest claim query: MATCH (c:Claim) RETURN c.created_date ORDER BY c.created_date DESC LIMIT 1
Use the driver session pattern: const session = driver.session(); try { const result = await session.run(cypher); } finally { await session.close(); }
Each query runs in its own session (short-lived sessions, not long-lived)
If any query fails, log error to stderr and show 'N/A' for that metric instead of crashing
Phase 1 criteria check uses real Claim count from Cypher, not mock
Output format unchanged — same console.log dashboard layout
Test: run cmdStatus against a Neo4j instance with known data → verify counts match MATCH queries
Test: run cmdStatus when Neo4j is down → verify graceful degradation (shows DISCONNECTED)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Status is the first command users run to verify the system. With 1,840 claims already in the graph, we should see real numbers immediately. The mock showed 'Person: 25, Claim: 196' etc. — after this story, it should show the actual counts. This is the fastest way to prove the wiring works.

userStories.title: Wire cmdPrivacyAudit to run real 6-point Cypher audit queries
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the privacy-audit command to run the actual 6 Cypher audit queries from KnowledgePrivacyAuditor (src/knowledge/privacy-auditor.ts) so that privacy violations are detected against the live graph. Depends on: US-600.
acceptanceCriteria: Import KnowledgePrivacyAuditor from src/knowledge/privacy-auditor.ts
Replace the mock auditResults array with real audit execution
Audit 1 — Sole Write Owner: MATCH (c:Claim) WHERE c.created_by <> 'knowledge_agent' RETURN count(c)
Audit 2 — Children's Data Protection (Harrison): MATCH (e:Entity {name: 'Harrison Lingle'})<-[:ABOUT]-(c:Claim) WHERE NOT c.protection_level = 'children_elevated' RETURN count(c)
Audit 3 — Children's Data Protection (Beckham): same pattern for Beckham Lingle
Audit 4 — Connector Scope (Drive): MATCH (s:Source) WHERE s.source_account = 'google_drive' AND NOT s.folder IN ['GIX', 'WE', 'Finance'] RETURN count(s)
Audit 5 — Cross-Domain Leakage (Health): MATCH (c:Claim {domain: 'health'})-[:SOURCED_FROM]->(s:Source) WHERE s.source_account NOT IN ['obsidian_vault', 'apple_health'] RETURN count(c)

Audit 6 — Agent Access Anomaly: MATCH (c:Claim) WHERE c.last_accessed_by IS NOT NULL AND c.last_accessed_by <> 'knowledge_agent' RETURN c.last_accessed_by AS agent, c.domain AS domain, count(c) AS count
Non-zero results on Audits 1-5 trigger Level 3 escalation (same warning format)
Audit names in output match the Implementation Guide names, not the mock names
Results written to privacy_audit_log PostgreSQL table if operational-tables have been created
Test: run audit against clean graph → verify all 6 pass
Test: insert a Claim with created_by <> 'knowledge_agent' → run Audit 1 → verify detection and FAIL output
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Privacy is non-negotiable. The mock audit had generic names ('Unencrypted PII Exposure', 'SSN Field Leakage') that don't match our actual audit queries. The real audits are about sole write owner, children's data protection (Harrison and Beckham), connector scope, cross-domain leakage, and access anomalies. These are the enforcement of Charter Principle 7.

userStories.title: Wire cmdMigrate to use real vault parser, entity mappers, and decomposition pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the migrate command to use the real VaultConnector, FrontmatterParser, all 6 entity mappers, ClaimDecomposer, ContradictionDetector, and ConnectionDiscovery modules so that running 'npm run knowledge:migrate' actually migrates vault notes into Neo4j. Depends on: US-600, US-606.
acceptanceCriteria: Import VaultConnector from src/knowledge/vault-connector.ts
Import FrontmatterParser from src/knowledge/migration/parser.ts
Import all 6 mappers: EntityMapper, PersonMapper, AccountMapper, InvestmentMapper, CashFlowMapper, InstitutionMapper
Import ClaimDecomposer from src/knowledge/decomposition/decomposer.ts
Import ContradictionDetector from src/knowledge/decomposition/contradiction.ts
Import ConnectionDiscovery from src/knowledge/decomposition/connections.ts
Import EmbeddingPipeline from src/knowledge/embedding.ts
Step 1 (initNeo4j): already wired by US-600
Step 2 (applySchema): import and call applySchema from neo4j.ts to ensure schema/constraints exist
Step 3 (vault connect): use the VaultConnector wired by US-606 (do NOT instantiate a new one — reuse the initialized instance)
Step 4 (parse notes): use VaultConnector to list all markdown files, pass each through FrontmatterParser to detect template type
Step 5 (run mappers): route each parsed note to the correct mapper based on template type (entity→EntityMapper, person→PersonMapper, etc.), collect aggregate statistics
Step 6 (claim decomposition): for unstructured notes (no recognized template type), run through ClaimDecomposer
Step 7 (contradiction detection): run ContradictionDetector on newly created claims
Step 8 (connection discovery): run ConnectionDiscovery to find cross-domain relationships
Step 9 (summary): print real statistics from mapper/decomposer return values, not Math.random()
Step 10 (validate): check Phase 1 thresholds against real counts from Neo4j queries
Remove ALL Math.random() calls and mock variables
Each mapper/decomposer failure is logged but does not crash the pipeline (non-blocking, continue to next note)
Progress logging: emit [MIGRATE] progress every 50 notes processed
Test: run migrate against a test vault directory with 5 mixed-type notes → verify nodes created in Neo4j
Test: run migrate twice → verify MERGE idempotency (no duplicates)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the biggest story — migrate is the most complex command. The key insight is that ALL the real logic already exists in the v3.4 modules. This story is purely about importing and calling those modules in the right order. The pipeline should be resilient — if one note fails to parse, log it and move on. With ~300+ notes in the vault, a few failures are expected. IMPORTANT: Reuse the VaultConnector instance initialized by US-606 — do not create a new one.

userStories.title: Wire cmdQuery to use real AQM 4-stage pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the query command to run questions through the real AQMPipeline (all 4 stages) so that 'npm run knowledge:query "question"' returns grounded answers from the knowledge graph. Depends on: US-600.
acceptanceCriteria: Import AQMPipeline from src/knowledge/aqm/pipeline.ts
Import EmbeddingPipeline from src/knowledge/embedding.ts
Import ModelRouter from src/knowledge/model-router.ts
Initialize AQMPipeline with Neo4j driver, EmbeddingPipeline, and ModelRouter
Pass the user's question string to AQMPipeline.run() (or equivalent method)
Print the synthesized answer from Stage 4 to stdout
Print confidence score to stdout
Print source citations in format: [Claim ID: {id} | truth_tier: {tier} | truth_score: {score}]
Print identified gaps (things the graph doesn't know) if any
Print timing breakdown: Stage 1 time, Stage 2 time, Stage 3 time, Stage 4 time, total time
Remove the entire mockAnswer object and mock output
If AQM pipeline returns no results, print 'No knowledge available for this question.' gracefully
Test: query a question about an entity known to exist in the graph → verify grounded answer with citations
Test: query a question about something NOT in the graph → verify graceful 'no knowledge' response
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the query interface. The AQM pipeline was one of the most heavily tested subsystems in v3.4 (US-510, US-511). All 4 stages are built and tested. This story just connects stdin question → AQM pipeline → stdout answer. The timing breakdown helps identify which stage is the bottleneck for future optimization.

userStories.title: Wire cmdWatch to use real DualWriteSync file watcher
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the watch command to use the real DualWriteSync module and VaultConnector's file watcher so that 'npm run knowledge:watch' detects vault changes and syncs them to Neo4j in real time. Depends on: US-600, US-606.
acceptanceCriteria: Import DualWriteSync from src/knowledge/sync/dual-write.ts
Import VaultConnector from src/knowledge/vault-connector.ts
Use the VaultConnector instance wired by US-606 (do NOT instantiate a new one — reuse the initialized instance)
Initialize DualWriteSync with Neo4j driver and VaultConnector instance
Start the watcher using DualWriteSync.start() (or equivalent method)
On file change detected: log [WATCH] event type (create/modify/delete), file path, and timestamp to stderr
On sync completion: log [SYNC] result (success/failure), latency in ms, node IDs affected to stderr
Graceful shutdown: SIGINT and SIGTERM handlers call DualWriteSync.stop() and driver.close()
Watcher filters: only watch .md files, ignore .obsidian/ directory, ignore _agent_insights/ for Obsidian→Neo4j direction
Remove '// In production:' comments and mock infinite wait
The process stays alive until SIGINT/SIGTERM (correct — this is a long-running daemon)
Test: start watcher, create a .md file in test vault directory → verify Neo4j node created
Test: start watcher, send SIGINT → verify clean shutdown (no hanging process)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The watcher is the daemon that keeps Obsidian and Neo4j in sync. It should feel invisible — Mike edits a note in Obsidian, and within 5 seconds the graph reflects the change. The _agent_insights/ exclusion prevents infinite loops (agent writes to Obsidian → triggers sync → agent writes again). IMPORTANT: Reuse the VaultConnector instance from US-606 — do not create a new one.

userStories.title: Remove all mock code and '// In production:' comments from cli.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all remnants of mock/placeholder code removed from cli.ts so that the file is clean production code with no dead comments or Math.random() artifacts. Depends on: US-600 through US-606.
acceptanceCriteria: Zero occurrences of Math.random() in cli.ts
Zero occurrences of '// In production:' in cli.ts
Zero occurrences of 'Mock' or 'mock' in cli.ts (case-insensitive) except in error messages that genuinely reference mock data
Zero occurrences of 'placeholder' in cli.ts
All import statements at the top of the file reference real modules from src/knowledge/
No unused imports remain
Every function has proper TypeScript return types (no 'any' types except where Neo4j driver requires it)
All console.error logging uses consistent [TAG] prefix format
File header comment updated to say 'v3.5' instead of 'v3.4'
Test: grep cli.ts for 'random|mock|placeholder|In production' → verify 0 matches
Typecheck passes across entire src/knowledge/ directory
userStories.notes: This is the cleanup story. After all the wiring is done, sweep the file for any artifacts of the scaffold phase. The version bump to v3.5 in the header comment signals that this is the production-ready CLI, not the prototype.

userStories.title: End-to-end CLI validation: status, audit, and query against live Neo4j
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests that validate all 5 CLI commands work end-to-end against a real Neo4j instance so that we have confidence the wiring is correct before deploying on IronClaw. Depends on: US-600 through US-607.
acceptanceCriteria: Test 1 — Status: run 'npx tsx src/knowledge/cli.ts status' → verify CONNECTED, real node counts returned (Claim count should be ≥1000 based on existing 1,840 claims)
Test 2 — Privacy Audit: run 'npx tsx src/knowledge/cli.ts privacy-audit' → verify all 6 audit queries execute without error
Test 3 — Query: run 'npx tsx src/knowledge/cli.ts query "Who is Harrison Lingle?"' → verify grounded answer returned with citations
Test 4 — Query graceful degradation: run query with Neo4j down → verify error message, not crash
Test 5 — Status shows Phase 1 PASS when ≥100 claims exist (should be true with 1,840 claims)
Test 6 — All commands return correct exit codes: 0 for success, 1 for error, 2 for partial success
Test 7 — Typecheck: npx tsc --noEmit passes for src/knowledge/cli.ts and all imported modules
All tests can run in CI with a Neo4j test container (docker-compose with neo4j:5.26.0-community)
Test results logged to stdout in structured format for Ralph verification
userStories.notes: This is the gate check. If all 7 tests pass, the CLI is ready to deploy on IronClaw. The Harrison Lingle query is a good test because he's a Person entity that should exist in the graph from the initial migration. The Neo4j-down test proves the non-blocking error handling works end-to-end.
